entities.name: TAKUSA DISTRICT
entities.description: District in north Gondar zone, North Western Ethiopia, with high potential for double cropping

entities.name: NORTH WESTERN ETHIOPIA
entities.description: Region where the study on wheat-chickpea double cropping was conducted

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: Type of soil found in Takusa district where the experiments were established

entities.name: DOUBLE CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two crops in one growing season to maximize yield and benefit per unit area

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: Cereal crop used in the double cropping system, specifically bread wheat varieties

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Legume crop used in the double cropping system

entities.name: SENKEGNA
entities.description: A variety of bread wheat evaluated in the study

entities.name: TAY
entities.description: A variety of bread wheat evaluated in the study

entities.name: DINKNESH
entities.description: A variety of bread wheat that showed shortest days to mature and highest grain yield

entities.name: HABRU
entities.description: A variety of chickpea evaluated in the study

entities.name: NATOLI
entities.description: A variety of chickpea that gave higher yield and high MRR when double cropped with Dinknesh wheat

entities.name: RANDOMIZED COMPLETE BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: Experimental design used for the trials, laid down in a factorial arrangement with three replications

entities.name: DAYS TO MATURE
entities.description: The time taken for the crop to reach maturity, used to compare wheat varieties

entities.name: THOUSAND SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of one thousand seeds, used as a performance metric for wheat varieties

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, used to evaluate crop performance

entities.name: MARGINAL RATE OF RETURN
entities.description: An economic indicator used to evaluate the technical and economic feasibility of double cropping

entities.name: LAND EQUIVALENT RATION
entities.description: A measure used to demonstrate the rewards of double cropping in terms of yield and benefit maximization
